Shared Conversation
This shared conversation is generated by AI, for reference only.
What is the boiling point of water at sea level?

The user is asking a basic physics fact.

At sea level, water boils at 100 degrees Celsius (212 degrees Fahrenheit).

The boiling point drops as altitude increases because atmospheric pressure decreases.

And at the top of Mount Everest?

Near the summit of Mount Everest water boils at about 68 degrees Celsius (154 degrees Fahrenheit).
